title: No-Code AI Builder: Automate Anything

modules.title: What Workflows Are and Why They Break
modules.sub: Spotting the hidden chaos inside everyday manual processes
modules.description: Learners audit five real student council processes to identify where time is lost, errors creep in, and handoffs fail. They map each process as a workflow diagram and rank them by how broken — and how fixable — each one actually is.

modules.title: Connect the Pieces: Triggers, Actions, Logic
modules.sub: Building your first automation from triggers to outcomes
modules.description: Learners use a no-code tool to wire together triggers, actions, and conditional logic to automate one of the student council processes they identified. Every connection requires a decision, and every decision means accepting a trade-off they'll need to justify later.

modules.title: Add AI to a Real Automation You Built
modules.sub: Choosing where AI earns its place in your workflow
modules.description: Learners extend their existing automation by inserting at least one AI action — such as summarising responses, classifying requests, or drafting replies — and test whether it actually improves the workflow. They stress-test against messy, unexpected inputs and revise their design when things break.

modules.title: Launch and Defend Your Workflow Design
modules.sub: Presenting your choices, not just your finished product
modules.description: Learners finalise and document their automated workflow, then present it to peers with a clear rationale for every major design decision — including what they tried and abandoned. They field challenge questions and reflect honestly on what their automation still cannot handle.

synopsis: Your school's student council has five chaotic, manual processes that are eating up everyone's time — and you've been handed access to no-code AI tools and two weeks to fix as many as you can. Every design choice has a trade-off you'll have to live with.
rationale: No-code AI workflow tools are already reshaping entry-level work, and teens who can design, connect, and critically evaluate automated systems will have a significant practical advantage within years of leaving school.
learning_outcome: Students can design and build a multi-step automated workflow that incorporates at least one AI action, test it against failure cases, and present a clear rationale for the design decisions they made.
source_signals: Launch HN: AgentHub (YC W24) – A no-code automation platform
Show HN: I quit coding years ago. AI brought me back
How to learn AI for students & professionals (guide)
